---
title: "Week 12: The Surprise Detector"
subtitle: "Chapter 19"
date: "2026-11-10"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

Two full sessions on statistical inference: hypothesis testing, chi-square, correlation, regression, and APA reporting.

**Reading:** Chapter 19

## Key Concepts

- Null hypothesis significance testing
- P-values and common misinterpretations
- Chi-square test of independence
- Pearson correlation
- Simple linear regression
- Confidence intervals
- Statistical vs. practical significance
- APA reporting format

## Learning Objectives

1. Explain what a p-value does and does not tell you
2. Conduct chi-square, correlation, and regression in R
3. Report results in APA format with effect sizes
4. Distinguish statistical from practical significance

## Assignment: Describing Data [R] (75 pts)

Frequency tables, ggplot2 visualizations, cross-tabulations, and narrative interpretation.
